get.description:
List the org's catalogs (name, version, built_at, members).

Backs ``praxis ig list``.

schema.title: Response List Catalogs Ai Api Ig Catalogs Get

get.description:
Return the names of catalogs having a member with this canonical git URL.

Backs ``praxis ig claims --git <url>`` — the reverse lookup repo CI runs to
discover which catalogs claim it. CI authenticates via an API-key Bearer
token (``validate_user_cookie`` handles that). This literal path is declared
before ``/catalogs/{catalog}`` so it is not captured as a catalog name.
Return one catalog's summary; 404 if absent.

post.description:
Publish one member — its gzipped ``graph.json`` bytes plus ``git``/``sha``.

Idempotent by ``(org, catalog, member, sha)``: a repeat publish returns 200
with the same ``blob_id`` and creates no duplicate. Backs ``praxis ig
publish``, which repo CI calls with an API-key Bearer token.

After a successful publish we enqueue a background assemble for the catalog
(house ``BackgroundTasks`` idiom) so a published member leads to a fresh
catalog instead of a silently stale one. A concurrent assemble is rejected
by the runner and logged — the publish still returns 200 with its
``blob_id``.

get.description: Stream back a member's gzipped ``graph.json`` bytes; 404 if absent.

Stream the assembled catalog as a gzipped tarball for ``praxis ig sync``.

The ``ETag`` is the catalog version (from the stored summary). A caller that
already holds that version sends ``If-None-Match`` and gets a ``304`` with no
body, so an unchanged catalog is never re-downloaded.

post.description:
Rebuild this catalog's infra member (``ig infra build``) then re-assemble.

The infra member is the only member with no source repo, so no repo CI owns
it; it must be rebuilt server-side (it needs live Facets credentials) on
Facets deploy events and on a periodic safety net. This service does not
receive Facets deploy webhooks today, so this explicit endpoint is the manual
trigger (wiring a Facets deploy webhook to call it is a prerequisite for the
event-driven path).

Runs in the background (``ig infra build`` is slow); the trigger surfaces any
failure — including absent Facets credentials, which the runner reports
loudly before spawning ``ig`` — to the server logs.

Rebuild the infra member of every catalog keyed on a Facets project.

This is the event-driven trigger. The Facets control plane can already emit
a Facets-deploy webhook with no control-plane code change: an operator
creates one WEBHOOK notification channel whose ``authorizationHeader`` is
``Bearer <praxis api key>`` and subscribes ``APP_DEPLOYMENT`` to it, with a
Mustache ``payloadJson`` template that emits the deployed project. That
channel POSTs a single fixed URL, so this endpoint is *project*-keyed (not
catalog-keyed like the manual ops route above): the body carries the Facets
project and we fan out to every catalog in the caller's org whose manifest
keys its infra member on it.

Auth + org scoping are identical to the other ig routes: ``validate_user_cookie``
resolves the ``Authorization: Bearer <api-key>`` the webhook sends and the
org from the key's context. The fan-out never crosses orgs.

Response (always ``200``, even for a project no catalog names — a webhook
firing for an unrelated project must not error)::

    {
      "project": "<facets project>",
      "triggered": ["<catalog>", ...],   # infra refresh started
      "skipped":   [{"catalog": "<c>", "reason": "<why>"}]
    }

We reuse :func:`refresh_infra_for_catalog` (which reuses the assemble
runner's ``infra_refresh``) and await each attempt so the response reports
the real per-catalog outcome. A catalog whose assemble is already running is
reported in ``skipped`` (its runner returns a non-success result rather than
raising) — a normal outcome, never a 500.

Push a catalog's manifest text (push-only, no server-side editing).

The manifest is a small YAML file authored on a developer's laptop; ig
never owns its content. The server records the text verbatim plus stamps
``pushed_by`` (the caller), ``pushed_at``, and the client-supplied
``git_sha``. Backs ``praxis ig manifest push``.

get.description:
Pull a catalog's manifest; 404 if none has been pushed.

Backs ``praxis ig manifest pull``, which repo CI calls with an API-key
Bearer token.

This is the unified interface that all trigger sources convert to.
The ExternalAgentRunner accepts this and returns ExternalAgentResponse.

Response from running an agent via external trigger.

Contains the agent's output and metadata for the trigger source
to format and deliver appropriately.

question_id.title: Question Id

ExternalAgentResumeRequest.title: ExternalAgentResumeRequest
ExternalAgentResumeRequest.description:
Wrapper for resuming a suspended run on a worker pod.

resume_with_answer()'s signature isn't a single model, so this bundles its
arguments for the orchestrator->worker forward (POST /internal/
external-agent-resume). ``request`` is the original ExternalAgentRequest,
used both for agent context and to route to the same worker pod.

finding_key.description: Stable identity slug. Caller-supplied via record_finding or auto-derived as sha1(title|service|environment)[:16]. Two findings with the same (schedule_id, finding_key) are treated as the same recurring issue.

first_seen_run_id.title: First Seen Run Id
first_seen_run_id.description: Run id of the FIRST run where this finding_key surfaced.

last_seen_run_id.title: Last Seen Run Id
last_seen_run_id.description: Run id of the MOST RECENT run where this finding_key surfaced. Equal to the containing run's id when first written; updated by push_finding on subsequent runs.

recurrence_count.title: Recurrence Count
recurrence_count.description: Number of consecutive runs this finding_key has been open.

resolved_at.title: Resolved At
resolved_at.description: When status flipped to 'resolved'. None while open.

last_seen_at.title: Last Seen At
last_seen_at.description: started_at of the most recent run where this finding_key surfaced. Populated by list_findings_for_schedule only — absent on per-run reads.

Finding.title: Finding
Finding.description: A finding discovered during a scheduled agent run.

FindingStatus.title: FindingStatus
FindingStatus.description:
Lifecycle status of a recurring finding.

open      — currently observed; will be re-checked on next run
resolved  — auto-resolved after K clean runs OR explicitly closed
suppressed — user marked "ignore"; reserved for Phase 4 UI

objective_seed.title: Objective Seed
objective_seed.description: When provided, used verbatim as the objective. Otherwise the handler derives a short objective from the chat transcript.

GcpCredentials.title: GcpCredentials
GcpCredentials.description: GCP credentials for cross-project access via Service Account.

Auth mode determines how the schedule authenticates:
- 'pat': Uses a stored PAT (decrypted at execution time)
- 'github_app_installation': Uses the org's GitHub App installation token (generated per run)

enterprise_hostname.title: Enterprise Hostname

GitPATCreate.description: Schema for creating a new Git PAT

GitPATListResponse.title: GitPATListResponse
GitPATListResponse.description: Response model for listing Git PATs

GitPATResponse.title: GitPATResponse
GitPATResponse.description: Schema for Git PAT API responses (without exposing token)

GitPATUpdate.title: GitPATUpdate
GitPATUpdate.description: Schema for updating an existing Git PAT

IgCatalogSummary.title: IgCatalogSummary
IgCatalogSummary.description:
The catalog manifest — ``ig``'s ``metadata.json`` — without member blobs.

The real ``ig`` binary writes the catalog name under the key ``catalog``
(alongside ``schema_version`` / ``ig_version`` / ``routes``, which we ignore).
Older/synthesized metadata and internal callers use ``name``. We accept
either via a validation alias so ``IgCatalogSummary(**metadata_json)`` parses
ig's output verbatim, while ``populate_by_name`` keeps ``name=`` construction
working. Serialization always emits ``name`` (no serialization alias is set),
which the HTTP API and the CLI depend on.

IgClaimsResponse.title: IgClaimsResponse
IgClaimsResponse.description:
Reverse lookup: which catalogs claim a given canonical git URL.

Backs ``praxis ig claims --git <url>`` — the query repo CI runs to discover
which catalogs embed it.

IgInfraRefreshRequest.title: IgInfraRefreshRequest
IgInfraRefreshRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /ig/infra/refresh`` — the project-keyed infra refresh.

A Facets deploy webhook (a WEBHOOK notification channel subscribed to
``APP_DEPLOYMENT``) POSTs a JSON body rendered from a Mustache template the
operator authors. Since the template author picks the field name, we accept
either ``project`` or ``facets_project``; exactly one must be present and
non-empty (both, or neither, is a 422). :attr:`resolved_project` is the
trimmed value.

IgManifest.title: IgManifest
IgManifest.description:
A catalog's ``ig`` manifest — the small YAML text authored on a laptop.

``ig`` never owns the manifest's content; the server only stores the text
verbatim plus provenance stamps. ``pushed_by`` is the caller's email,
``pushed_at`` the server-side push time, and ``git_sha`` the client-supplied
commit the manifest came from. Push-only: there is no server-side editing.

IgMemberBlobRef.title: IgMemberBlobRef
IgMemberBlobRef.description:
A pointer to one stored member blob (gzipped ``graph.json`` in GridFS).

Returned by ``IgCatalogStore.put_member``. ``blob_id`` is the stringified
GridFS file id; ``digest`` is the ``sha256`` hex of the gzipped bytes.

IgMemberMeta.title: IgMemberMeta
IgMemberMeta.description:
One member of a catalog, as recorded in ``ig``'s ``member-meta.json``.

``git`` / ``sha`` are the canonical identity of a ``code`` member. ``infra``
members (the project's Facets graph) have no repo, so both are ``None``.

ImportConflictInfo.title: ImportConflictInfo
ImportConflictInfo.description: Information about name conflicts during import

ImportPreviewResponse.title: ImportPreviewResponse
ImportPreviewResponse.description: Preview response when importing an agent

ImportResponse.title: ImportResponse
ImportResponse.description: Response after successfully importing an agent

InboundConnection.title: InboundConnection
InboundConnection.description: An edge pointing at the requested repo from another repo.

IncidentChannelConfigCreate.title: IncidentChannelConfigCreate
IncidentChannelConfigCreate.description: Schema for creating/updating incident channel configuration.

enabled_for_chat.title: Enabled For Chat
enabled_for_chat.description: Usable from an interactive chat starter

enabled_for_schedule.title: Enabled For Schedule
enabled_for_schedule.description: Usable from a scheduled (unattended) agent run

schedule_blocker.title: Schedule Blocker
schedule_blocker.description: Human-readable reason scheduling is unavailable, if any

IntegrationGate.title: IntegrationGate
IntegrationGate.description:
Per-integration availability split by consumer context.

Some integrations (notably per-user PATs) are safe to use in an
interactive chat — the user is present to consent to the token —
but unsafe in a scheduled/cron run where no human is in the loop.
Consumers gate accordingly.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Integration keys with at least one active connection

disabled.title: Disabled
disabled.description: Shippable integration keys with no active connection

unavailable.title: Unavailable
unavailable.description: Integration keys not yet shippable in the product

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.description: Per-key availability split by consumer (chat vs schedule). Most integrations are uniformly available in both contexts; VCS PATs (gitlab, bitbucket, and the synthetic 'code-repo' key) differ because they're per-user credentials.

IntegrationsStatusResponse.title: IntegrationsStatusResponse
IntegrationsStatusResponse.description: Per-org connection state for every integration the catalog knows.

InvestigationStatus.title: InvestigationStatus
InvestigationStatus.description: AI investigation status (separate from incident lifecycle).

extra_instructions.title: Extra Instructions
extra_instructions.description: Optional one-shot instructions for this run (free text)

InvokeRequest.title: InvokeRequest
InvokeRequest.description:
Request body for firing an agent webhook.

`extra_instructions` is plain-English instructions for this run
only — appended to the webhook's stored objective. Empty body is
fine; the webhook will run on the stored objective alone.

InvokeResponse.title: InvokeResponse
InvokeResponse.description:
Response from firing an agent webhook.

The webhook runs asynchronously in the background. The caller gets
a `run_id` immediately and can poll for status / artifacts via the
authenticated management API.

K8sActivityPatch.title: K8sActivityPatch
K8sActivityPatch.description:
Partial update to K8s activity metadata.

Only fields the user is allowed to set from outside the discovery
flow are exposed. Status transitions go through dedicated discovery
endpoints, not this PATCH.

K8sActivityState.title: K8sActivityState
K8sActivityState.description:
Embedded K8s migration activity state on a parent MigrationProject.

Present on a MigrationProject iff the user has started the K8s
Migration activity for that project. Identity (id, name, description,
organization_id, created_by_email) lives on the parent.

Workload/Helm/wiring scan results stay in the
`k8s_discovery_configs` collection, keyed by the parent
MigrationProject.id.

memory is about. We deliberately picked `audience` over `scope` to
avoid colliding with `kind=user` (which means "ABOUT the user").

MemoryCategory.description: Legacy taxonomy — kept for back-compat. New code should read `kind`.

Request body for sending a message via a chat integration.

SkillCreate.title: SkillCreate
SkillCreate.description: Schema for creating a new skill

SkillFile.title: SkillFile
SkillFile.description:
One supporting file of a multi-file ("nested") skill.

A skill is a directory: ``SKILL.md`` lives in ``Skill.content`` and every
other file in the skill folder is a ``SkillFile`` carried alongside. ``path``
is the skill-relative POSIX path (e.g. ``nuances-catalog.md`` or
``flows/onboard.md``); installers recreate it verbatim under the skill dir.

scope.description: Destination scope for the imported skill

surface.description: Runnable surface for the imported skill (cli, ui, both)

SkillImport.title: SkillImport
SkillImport.description:
Request body for the paste-markdown import endpoint.

The Knowledge → Skills page lets users paste a Claude-style skill
markdown blob (YAML frontmatter + body). Only the markdown text and
the destination scope cross the wire — the route parses the
frontmatter and turns it into a SkillCreate.

SkillSurface.title: SkillSurface
SkillSurface.description:
Where a skill is meant to run — its *authored intent*, not tool-resolvability.

This is orthogonal to the CLI installability gate
(``CLI_INSTALLABLE_GLOBAL_SKILLS`` in ``routes/cli_skills.py``): that gate
answers "can this GLOBAL skill's tools be satisfied on a local host?", while
``surface`` answers "which surface is this skill *for*?".

- ``CLI``  — local AI host only (drives local binaries like ``raptor`` / ``aws``
  or a local browser). Excluded from the server-hosted UI agent's skill sync
  (``anthropic_agents/skills/skill_sync.py``) because the UI has no terminal.
- ``UI``   — server-hosted chat only. Excluded from the CLI ``/bundle``.
- ``BOTH`` — runs on either surface (default; backward-compatible for every
  existing skill).

SkillUpdate.title: SkillUpdate
SkillUpdate.description:
Schema for updating an existing skill (partial updates).

Permissive on minimums and any field where existing rows could violate
a tighter Create-only constraint. Only max_length caps apply so legacy
rows that violate Create-only minimums remain editable.

SlackBotCreate.title: SlackBotCreate
SlackBotCreate.description: Schema for creating a Slack Bot.

SlackBotListResponse.title: SlackBotListResponse
SlackBotListResponse.description: API response for listing Slack Bots.

SlackBotUpdate.title: SlackBotUpdate
SlackBotUpdate.description: Schema for updating a Slack Bot.

SlackChannelConfigResponse.title: SlackChannelConfigResponse
SlackChannelConfigResponse.description: Response for workspace Slack channel configuration.

reply_broadcast.title: Reply Broadcast
reply_broadcast.description: Also post to channel when replying in thread

SlackSendPayload.title: SlackSendPayload
SlackSendPayload.description:
Whitelisted Slack chat.postMessage payload.

Only safe fields are accepted. Impersonation fields (as_user, username,
icon_emoji, icon_url), token, and deprecated attachments are blocked.

TargetType.title: TargetType
TargetType.description:
Type of infrastructure target.

Matches the k8s_sources pattern from incident ops:
- facets_env: Facets CP environment (resolved via FacetsIntegration PAT)
- k8s_integration: Org-level K8s integration (direct kubeconfig)
- cloud_integration: Org-level cloud integration (AWS/GCP/Azure) — TODO: not yet used
